description: Database schema: organizations, users, org_memberships, projects, deployments, pages, api_keys, agent_jobs, assistant_conversations, analytics_events, audit_logs tables with Drizzle ORM
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Run `make db-push` to sync schema to RDS Postgres. All tables created with proper foreign keys, indexes, and constraints.
data_model: See build-spec.md Data Models section for all field types

description: S3 bucket configuration for file uploads (images, media, doc assets) with CORS and presigned URL generation
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Upload files via presigned URLs, serve via S3 or CloudFront
data_model: Files stored in S3 with path: /{orgId}/{projectId}/assets/*

description: Better Auth setup with Google OAuth provider, session management in Postgres, auth middleware for protected routes
ui_details: Login page with Google OAuth button, centered card layout
behavior: Click Google OAuth → redirect to Google → callback → session created → redirect to dashboard. Middleware protects all /dashboard/* routes.
data_model: Better Auth manages user, session, account tables

description: Organization creation during signup, org membership with roles (admin/editor/viewer)
ui_details: After first login, prompt to create org with name input
behavior: First-time user creates org → becomes admin. Subsequent users can be invited to existing orgs.
data_model: organizations(id, name, slug, plan, settings, createdAt), org_memberships(id, orgId, userId, role, createdAt)

description: API key management — generate admin (mint_) and assistant (mint_dsc_) prefixed keys, store hashed, list/revoke
ui_details: Table of API keys with name, prefix, created date, last used. Create button opens modal with name input. Key shown once on creation.
behavior: Create key → generate random token with prefix → hash and store → show full key once. List shows masked keys. Delete revokes.
data_model: api_keys(id, orgId, name, keyPrefix, keyHash, type, createdAt, lastUsedAt)

description: Dashboard layout shell: sidebar navigation with org switcher, project selector, nav groups (Overview, Editor, Analytics, Products, Settings), top bar with user avatar/menu
ui_details: Left sidebar ~240px, dark/light mode. Logo at top, nav groups with icons, user menu at bottom. Main content area with breadcrumbs.
behavior: Sidebar collapses on mobile. Active nav item highlighted. User menu dropdown with logout.
data_model: N/A

description: Project CRUD — create documentation project with name, connect GitHub repo (optional), configure subdomain
ui_details: Project creation form: name, repo URL (optional), subdomain preview. Settings page shows project details with edit forms.
behavior: Create project → generate subdomain → save to DB. Edit name/settings. Cannot delete org's last project.
data_model: projects(id, orgId, name, slug, repoUrl, repoBranch, repoPath, customDomain, subdomain, settings, status, createdAt, updatedAt)

description: Page management — create, read, update, delete MDX documentation pages within a project
ui_details: File tree sidebar showing page hierarchy. Create page modal with path/title. Delete with confirmation.
behavior: CRUD operations on pages. Pages stored in DB with MDX content, frontmatter, path. Tree reflects docs.json navigation.
data_model: pages(id, projectId, path, title, description, content, frontmatter, isPublished, createdAt, updatedAt)

description: MDX editor with syntax highlighting — code editor pane for editing MDX content with basic toolbar (bold, italic, heading, link, image, code block)
ui_details: Split pane: left = code editor (CodeMirror/Monaco with MDX syntax), right = live preview. Toolbar above editor.
behavior: Type MDX → see rendered preview in real-time. Toolbar inserts markdown syntax. Auto-save draft.
data_model: Uses pages table content field

description: MDX renderer — render MDX content to HTML with Mintlify component library (Callouts, Cards, Tabs, Steps, Code Groups, Accordions, etc.)
ui_details: Docs site layout: left sidebar nav + center content + right panel (TOC). Components styled per Mintlify defaults.
behavior: MDX content parsed and rendered with custom components. Navigation from docs.json. Table of contents auto-generated from headings.
data_model: Reads pages table + project settings (docs.json)

description: Deployment system — trigger deployment, build status tracking, deployment history list
ui_details: Deployment list: table with status badge (queued/building/succeeded/failed), commit SHA, timestamp. Overview shows latest deployment status.
behavior: Trigger deployment → status updates in real-time. Git push webhook triggers auto-deploy. History shows all deployments.
data_model: deployments(id, projectId, status, commitSha, commitMessage, startedAt, endedAt, createdAt)

description: Dashboard overview page — deployment status, site URL, quick action cards (edit, settings, analytics)
ui_details: Hero section with site URL and status. Quick links: Open editor, View site, Settings. Recent deployments list.
behavior: Shows current deployment status. Click site URL opens docs in new tab. Quick links navigate to respective pages.
data_model: Reads project + latest deployment

description: Analytics dashboard — page views chart, top pages table, unique visitors, search queries, feedback summary
ui_details: Date range picker at top. Charts: line chart for views over time. Tables: top pages by views, search queries with counts, feedback summary.
behavior: Select date range → data updates. Export to CSV. Filter by page. Shows human vs AI traffic split.
data_model: analytics_events aggregated by type, page, date

description: AI Assistant — chat widget on docs site that answers questions from indexed documentation content with source citations
ui_details: Floating chat button bottom-right. Opens chat panel with message input, conversation history, source citations as links.
behavior: User asks question → AI searches indexed docs → streams response with cited sources. Conversation persists in session.
data_model: assistant_conversations(id, projectId, messages, createdAt). Pages indexed for semantic search.

description: Team management — invite members by email, assign roles, remove members, member list
ui_details: Table of members: avatar, name, email, role badge, joined date, actions (edit role, remove). Invite button opens modal with email + role select.
behavior: Invite → email sent → user clicks link → joins org. Change role via dropdown. Remove with confirmation. Only admins can invite admins.
data_model: org_memberships + users table

description: Agent management — create agent jobs from prompts, view job status and PR links, send follow-up messages
ui_details: Prompt input at top. Job list: status badge, prompt preview, PR link, timestamps. Job detail: full prompt, messages, PR diff preview.
behavior: Enter prompt → agent creates job → processes in background → creates PR. Follow-up messages refine the output. Status updates in real-time.
data_model: agent_jobs(id, projectId, prompt, status, prUrl, messages, createdAt, updatedAt)

description: Project appearance settings — theme colors, logo upload (light/dark), favicon, dark mode toggle
ui_details: Color pickers for primary, background, accent. Logo upload dropzone (light + dark variants). Favicon upload. Dark mode selector.
behavior: Change color → preview updates. Upload logo → stored in S3. Save triggers redeployment.
data_model: project.settings.appearance JSON

description: Navigation configuration — visual editor for docs.json navigation structure (groups, pages, tabs, anchors)
ui_details: Drag-and-drop tree editor for navigation groups and pages. Add/remove groups, reorder pages, set icons.
behavior: Drag to reorder → save updates docs.json navigation. Add group opens modal with name + icon. Changes reflected in docs site after deploy.
data_model: project.settings.navigation JSON

description: Custom domain configuration — add custom domain, DNS verification instructions, TLS certificate
ui_details: Domain input field. After adding: shows DNS CNAME record to add, verification status (pending/verified/failed).
behavior: Enter domain → generate CNAME target → show instructions. Verify button checks DNS. Once verified, site accessible at custom domain.
data_model: project.customDomain, project.settings.domain

description: REST API — deployment endpoints: POST /api/v1/project/update/{projectId} (trigger), GET /api/v1/project/update-status/{statusId}
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Bearer token auth with admin API key. Trigger returns statusId. Status returns deployment progress. Rate limited.
data_model: Uses deployments table

description: REST API — assistant endpoints: POST /api/v1/assistant/create-message (streaming), GET /api/v1/assistant/search, GET /api/v1/assistant/get-page-content
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Assistant key auth. Create-message streams AI response. Search returns matching pages. Get-page-content returns full MDX.
data_model: Uses pages + assistant_conversations tables

description: REST API — analytics export endpoints: GET views, visitors, feedback, searches, assistant-conversations, assistant-caller-stats, feedback-by-page
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Admin key auth. All accept dateFrom/dateTo query params. Return JSON arrays. Paginated.
data_model: Uses analytics_events table aggregations

description: REST API — agent endpoints: POST create-agent-job, GET get-agent-job, POST send-message
ui_details: N/A
behavior: Admin key auth. Create job triggers background processing. Get returns status + PR link. Send-message adds follow-up instruction.
data_model: Uses agent_jobs table

description: Onboarding flow — step-by-step wizard: create org → connect GitHub (skippable) → create project → first deployment
ui_details: Multi-step wizard with progress indicator. Step 1: org name. Step 2: GitHub connection (with skip option). Step 3: project name + subdomain. Step 4: deployment success screen.
behavior: Linear wizard flow. Each step validates before proceeding. Skip GitHub → auto-create repo. Final step shows live site URL.
data_model: Creates organization, project, first deployment records

description: Empty states for dashboard pages — analytics (no data yet), agent (connect integrations), editor (template content)
ui_details: Centered illustration + text + CTA button for each empty state. Analytics: 'No data yet, share your docs URL'. Agent: 'Connect Slack or Linear'.
behavior: Empty states shown when no data exists. CTA buttons navigate to relevant setup pages.
data_model: N/A — conditional rendering based on data existence

description: Docs site layout — left sidebar navigation, center content area, right panel (table of contents), top bar with search + theme toggle
ui_details: 3-column layout: sidebar ~260px, content ~720px, right panel ~240px. Responsive: sidebar collapses to hamburger on mobile. Search modal with keyboard shortcut.
behavior: Sidebar shows navigation from docs.json. Active page highlighted. Right panel auto-generates TOC from headings. Theme toggle switches light/dark.
data_model: Reads project.settings.navigation + page headings

description: Interactive API playground — send real API requests from docs, show request/response, auth header pre-fill from OpenAPI spec
ui_details: Right panel shows request builder: method badge, URL, headers, body editor. Send button. Response area with status code, body, headers.
behavior: Auto-populate from OpenAPI spec. User fills parameters → click Send → real HTTP request → show response. Auth header pre-filled if configured.
data_model: Reads OpenAPI spec from project settings

description: Docs site search — full-text search across all pages with results ranked by relevance
ui_details: Cmd+K opens search modal. Input at top, results below grouped by section. Each result: title, description snippet, breadcrumb path.
behavior: Type query → instant results (debounced). Click result → navigate to page. Recent searches shown when empty.
data_model: Postgres full-text search on pages table (title, content, description)

description: User feedback widget on docs pages — thumbs up/down rating, optional text feedback
ui_details: "Was this helpful?" with thumbs up/down buttons. After clicking, optional text input for details. Toast confirmation.
behavior: Click thumbs up/down → record feedback → show thank you. Optional text submitted separately. Feedback visible in dashboard analytics.
data_model: analytics_events with type='feedback', data={rating, text, pageId}
